message:
test(readme): cover Series.apply / map / map_func (fd90.235 / br-vgj04)

Extends readme_element_wise_operations_compiles_and_runs to cover the
3 Series transform methods at fp-frame:3388-3453 that previously had
no integration coverage:

- Series.apply (Fn(&Scalar) -> Scalar) — exercised with a closure that
  doubles Float64 values
- Series.map_func — alias for apply
- Series.map (&[(Scalar, Scalar)]) — pandas-style mapping with a
  slice of (from, to) pairs

(Series.map_with_na_action was already covered in fd90.184.)

Verified: 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -850,6 +850,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-v7oq","title":"fd90.62: fp-columnar needs crate-level rustdoc summarizing column storage","description":"Continuation of fd90.61. Add crate-level //! rustdoc to fp-columnar - the columnar storage layer used by fp-frame for DataFrame columns. Public surface: Column (the columnar value container), ColumnError, ValidityMask (bitmap for missing values), ArithmeticOp / ComparisonOp enums for typed binary operations. Doc should explain the role (column storage backing DataFrame), the relationship to fp-types (Scalar values), and the public API entry points. Pure docs. Verify with cargo doc -p fp-columnar --no-deps under RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:43:34.483386983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:45:30.411468146Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:45:30.408949699Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","fd90","review"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-v9po","title":"Add series_rolling_var/count and ewm_mean and resample_sum/mean/count edge cases","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:28:22.967980657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T20:29:15.952839036Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T20:29:15.951054829Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-vc9cp","title":"fd90.162: README random sampling example calls non-existent compute_weight(i) — replace with inline expression","description":"fd90.74-161 self-review. The Random Sampling example at line 1641 (added by fd90.115) has:

  let weights: Vec<f64> = (0..df.len()).map(|i| compute_weight(i)).collect();

The compute_weight function doesn't exist anywhere — it was a placeholder for 'user-defined weight function'. A user copy-pasting this hits:
  error[E0425]: cannot find function  in this scope

This is an outlier among the README's code examples — most others compile. Fix: replace with an inline weight expression (e.g. positional weighting where row index becomes the weight) so the example is genuinely runnable. Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T15:49:30.931231782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T15:50:47.796752498Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T15:50:47.793932948Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-vgj04","title":"fd90.235: Cover Series.apply / Series.map / Series.map_func","description":"fd90.235: Cover Series.apply / Series.map / Series.map_func.

Three Series transform methods at fp-frame:3388-3453 are uncovered:
- Series.apply<F: Fn(&Scalar) -> Scalar> (line 3388)
- Series.map_func<F> (line 3399, similar to apply)
- Series.map(&[(Scalar, Scalar)]) — pandas-style mapping with a slice of (from, to) pairs (line 3453)

Series.map_with_na_action is already covered (in fd90.184).

Scope:
- Extend an existing test with all 3 calls.

All 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-vhq2","title":"fd90.20: SqlConnection::list_tables for backend-agnostic table discovery","description":"Add SqlConnection::list_tables(schema: Option<&str>) -> Vec<String> trait method for backend-agnostic table discovery. Default impl returns empty Vec (backends that can't introspect). rusqlite impl uses 'SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type=table' filtered by schema (silently ignored on SQLite per supports_schemas==false). Free-fn list_sql_tables(conn, schema) wrapper exposes the API at fp-io top level. Tests cover empty DB, populated DB, schema-Some on SQLite (silently ignored), schema-Some on a multi-schema stub backend.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-25T20:21:10.294844156Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T20:24:29.991628127Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T20:24:29.989102037Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fd90","review","sql"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-vhr55","title":"fd90.231: Cover Series stat + inspection methods (skew/kurt/argmax/is_unique/etc.)","description":"fd90.231: Cover Series statistical / inspection methods.

Series has 11+ statistical and structural-inspection methods at fp-frame:3311-5233 with no integration coverage:

Higher-order statistics (return f64):
- skew, kurt, kurtosis, sem (lines 5038-5093)

Position lookups (return usize):
- argmax, argmin (lines 4788-4809)

Boolean inspection (return bool):
- is_unique, is_monotonic_increasing, is_monotonic_decreasing
- hasnans (already covered minimally)

Boolean reductions (return bool):
- all, any (lines 4073-4088)

Top/bottom-N:
- Series.nlargest(n) / Series.nsmallest(n) — already covered for DataFrame in fd90.185 but Series-level is not exercised

Scope:
- Extend an existing test (readme_element_wise_operations) with all calls.

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs.
Time estimate: 15-20min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-27T04:15:38.835085010Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T04:17:39.572768277Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T04:17:39.570539774Z","close_reason":"Extended readme_element_wise_operations_compiles_and_runs to cover 13 Series-level statistical and structural-inspection methods previously uncovered: skew/kurt/kurtosis/sem (4 f64 stats), argmax/argmin (2 usize positions), is_unique/is_monotonic_increasing/is_monotonic_decreasing (3 bool inspection), all/any (2 bool reductions on a Bool Series), nlargest(3)/nsmallest(2) (Series-level top-N). Built two helper Series — a 6-element float for stats and a 3-element bool for reductions. All 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-vic4","title":"CONFORMANCE: pandas Categorical parity matrix","description":"Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses: add 5-10 live pandas oracle parity cases for Categorical behavior across empty categoricals, single value, repeated categories, missing values, ordered categories, unused categories, and mixed label edge cases. Land any divergence as [HIGH] review bead with failing input + actual + expected.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T03:22:23.274748313Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T05:51:52.406285175Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T05:51:52.404434840Z","close_reason":"Added 8 live-oracle Categorical parity matrix cases and wired categorical oracle payload support","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["categorical","conformance","parity-matrix"]}
